title: Semantics and Pragmatics
description: Encoded meaning, contextual meaning, inference, stance, politeness, ambiguity, and discourse interpretation.
upgradePremise: Expanded for Nyx and Marion with stronger grammatical reasoning, case-based analysis, modules, projects, and explicit source coverage.

forNyx: Prefer precise, grammatical phrasing that sounds natural rather than stiff.
Use register-sensitive wording so conversational output and formal output do not collapse into one style.
When uncertain, preserve meaning first, then improve form through revision rather than over-correcting.
forMarion: Use the pack to explain why a wording choice works, not just whether it is 'right' or 'wrong'.
Ground revision advice in form-function reasoning, register, audience, and evidence.
Support coaching, diagnostics, and portfolio-style improvement tracking.

topics.name: Semantic Relations
coreClaims: Meaning relations include entailment, contradiction, presupposition, and ambiguity.
Ambiguity can be structural (syntax) or lexical (word meaning).
practice: Test entailment with paraphrase and negation.
Identify ambiguity type and propose disambiguations.

topics.name: Pragmatics and Implicature
coreClaims: Speakers imply more than they state; hearers infer based on norms and context.
Politeness, hedging, and stance are pragmatic tools heavily used in academic writing.
practice: Rewrite a claim with calibrated hedging appropriate for academic prose.
Explain a misunderstanding as a pragmatic mismatch (not just 'bad grammar').

modules.name: Implicature and Inference
outcomes: Explain what is suggested rather than stated.

modules.name: Hedging, Politeness, and Stance
outcomes: Tune directness to relationship and task.
Prevent pragmatic mismatch in academic and advisory writing.

caseStudies.title: Technically Correct, Socially Wrong
caseStudies.scenario: An answer is semantically accurate but pragmatically harsh or confusing.

caseStudies.title: Ambiguity in User Prompts
caseStudies.scenario: A user request permits multiple interpretations and the assistant must infer cautiously without hallucinating.
intervention: Ask only when ambiguity materially changes the task.

projects.name: Inference Guardrails
projects.goal: Create a small annotation set showing literal meaning, probable implied meaning, and safer assistant response behavior.

referenceTextsUsed.title: Style: Lessons in Clarity and Grace
referenceTextsUsed.citationAPA: Williams, J. M., Colomb, G. G., & Bizup, J. (2017). Style: Lessons in clarity and grace (12th ed.). Pearson.

referenceTextsUsed.title: Some Questions and Answers about Grammar
referenceTextsUsed.organization: National Council of Teachers of English

definitionTerms.definition: Semantics is the study of encoded meaning: what words, phrases, and sentences mean by linguistic structure.
Pragmatics is the study of contextual meaning: what speakers imply and listeners infer in real situations.
Ambiguity occurs when a word, phrase, sentence, or request allows more than one plausible interpretation.
Implicature is meaning suggested by context rather than directly stated.
Presupposition is background information a sentence treats as already accepted or assumed.

runtimeDefinitionIndex.fallbackRule: If term appears here, answer directly. Do not ask for more detail unless the user requests a specific register, example, or comparison.

rules: If the user asks 'what is', 'define', 'meaning of', or 'explain' plus a known English term, answer directly from this domain before generic fallback.
Do not route English definition questions into technical_debug unless the user explicitly asks for backend, code, file, audit, autopsy, patch, route, script, endpoint, deployment, or validation.
If a term overlaps with another domain, give a brief neutral definition, name the possible English interpretation, and ask one targeted clarification question.
Preserve meaning first; revise form second; never over-correct when the user asks for explanation rather than rewriting.
No user-facing generic fallback such as 'I need one more concrete detail' when the term is present in this pack. Use answer + clarification instead.
blockedFallbacks: I need one more concrete detail to route this cleanly.
I’ll keep this broad and practical
